description: Restore legacy no-profile collection merge behavior in the unified merge pipeline so repeat PUT and changed PUT flows keep stable collection row ordinals when the logical rowset is unchanged. Acceptance criteria: null-profile writes routed through `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWriteMerge.cs` no longer assign ordinals differently from the deleted `RelationalWriteNoProfileMerge.cs`; guarded no-op comparison remains true for unchanged rowsets; changed PUT flows reuse retained stable collection item ids without introducing ordinal churn or unnecessary `ContentVersion` bumps.
steps_to_verify: Add targeted unit coverage proving the unified null-profile collection merge produces the same merged ordinals and stable-row-id reuse as the legacy no-profile semantics for retained rows, changed rows, and large collections.

description: Harden the branch with explicit parity-style coverage for the no-profile path now that `NoProfileSyntheticProfileAdapter` feeds the shared merge/persist pipeline. Acceptance criteria: the branch contains tests that would fail if null-profile routing reintroduces ordinal drift, compare-order drift, or stable-id reuse regressions after future profile-aware merge changes.
steps_to_verify: Add unit tests for repeat PUT no-op, changed PUT retained-row updates, and large collection updates under null-profile routing.
Ensure at least one test asserts `ComparableCurrentRowset` and `ComparableMergedRowset` remain aligned for unchanged rowsets after ordering logic runs.

description: Fix profiled `VisibleAbsent` delete handling for separate-table non-collection scopes so the unified merge preserves scope-instance identity instead of collapsing deletes to a table-level sentinel. Acceptance criteria: collection-nested or otherwise multi-instance separate-table scopes delete only the intended visible scope instance; the merge carries enough concrete identity to bind `DeleteByParentSql` correctly for parent locator values beyond the root document id.
steps_to_verify: Refactor merge output and/or current-state lookup helpers so second-pass profiled deletes resolve the concrete current row for the specific `ScopeInstanceAddress`.
Add unit tests covering collection-nested `VisibleAbsent` separate-table deletion where sibling instances exist and only one may be deleted.

description: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalWritePersister.cs` so non-collection deletes bind from actual row identity values rather than assuming every `ParentKeyPart` equals the root `DocumentId`. Acceptance criteria: delete commands for root-adjacent scopes, collection-nested scopes, and other multi-parent-key shapes bind the correct parameter set for the target row.
steps_to_verify: Add persister-level tests for `DeleteByParentSql` with `ParentKeyPart(1+)` and collection-nested separate-table scopes.
Confirm existing root-adjacent delete tests still pass unchanged.

category: Profile Root Creatability And POST Target Coverage
description: Add the missing executor-level coverage for profile root creatability and final in-session POST target handling. Acceptance criteria: profiled POST create with `RootResourceCreatable=false` rejects before DML with the expected authorization failure; profiled POST-as-update does not trip the create-only root-creatability gate and flows onto the shared profiled update/no-op path; unchanged profiled POST-as-update is covered by guarded no-op tests.
steps_to_verify: Add executor tests for `CreateNew` plus `RootResourceCreatable=false`, and for POST target reevaluation resolving to `ExistingDocument` under profile context.
Add a guarded no-op test for unchanged profiled POST-as-update.

description: Close the story-level completeness gap by adding runtime integration/E2E coverage for the profiled shared executor path on real databases. Acceptance criteria: the temporary profile persist fence is replaced by positive runtime assertions; the profiled baseline scenarios required by `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/07-relational-write-path/03b-profile-aware-persist-executor.md` are exercised on PostgreSQL and SQL Server where the fixture matrix supports them.
steps_to_verify: Add integration or E2E coverage for profiled executor entry, root create rejection when non-creatable, unchanged profiled PUT and POST-as-update guarded no-op, and at least one nested profile-preservation/delete scenario against a real database.
Teardown and recreate the local DMS stack before E2E reruns per repo guidance if environment state is inconsistent.
Run the relevant PostgreSQL and SQL Server backend integration or E2E suites covering the profiled relational write path.
